chunk_summary: This section provides a worked example for staging IAP Class 2 and Class 3 obesity using standardized anthropometry and chart-based diagnosis, as per Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for IAP Class 2 and Class 3 obesity staging.
Learn to apply standardized anthropometric measurements in obesity diagnosis.
Interpret IAP growth charts for accurate obesity classification.
Familiarize with evidence-based guidelines for managing severe pediatric obesity.
key_facts: IAP Class 2 obesity is defined as BMI ≥ 120% of the 95th percentile for age and sex.
IAP Class 3 obesity is defined as BMI ≥ 140% of the 95th percentile for age and sex.
Standardized anthropometry includes height, weight, and BMI plotted on IAP growth charts.
The IAP Growth Charts 2015 are the recommended tool for pediatric obesity assessment in India.
Management of Class 2 and 3 obesity includes lifestyle interventions, dietary modifications, and, in severe cases, pharmacotherapy or bariatric surgery.
reference_quote: "IAP Class 2 obesity is defined as BMI ≥ 120% of the 95th percentile, while Class 3 obesity is defined as BMI ≥ 140% of the 95th percentile for age and sex." (Source: IAP guidelines on prevention and management of childhood obesity)
